name: RCA Document Creation (HTML)
query: Write an RCA for the authentication service outage we investigated
expected_behavior: Step 1: Determines the RCA file path (user-provided or /tmp/rca-<slug>.html)
Step 2: Reads the file if it exists (never writes without reading first)
Step 3: Determines action (new issue, correction, workaround, or false positive)
Step 4: Generates self-contained HTML following  docs visual standards
Step 5: Includes color-coded status badges, collapsible investigation, copy-to-clipboard on code
Step 6: Updates Runbook and Learnings sections in-place with transferable knowledge
Step 7: Saves to /tmp/ and tells user the path
success_criteria: Output is a single self-contained .html file with zero external dependencies
Always reads existing file before writing
Uses correct HTML structure (article.issue sections with status badges)
Fix and Runbook commands are in <pre> blocks with copy-to-clipboard
Appends Update blocks for corrections (never rewrites original content)
Runbook steps are copy-paste ready with verification steps
Learnings are transferable principles, not incident-specific observations
HTML opens correctly in a browser and is print-friendly
